topic: SAA Exponential Cell Separation — c8 change-driven review of post-c7 patches (tldr_verdict plain-text rewrite + detail_extra PRED-013 integration); audits c7 hole #1 closure scope across cohort
change_summary: tldr_verdict_length changed 368→254 (-31%) and detail_extra grew 0→228 chars since c7 (2026-05-29T07:15Z); commits bf3d721 (analyst-baby 2026-05-30T19:25Z, integrated EXP-477-stranded patches) and 819cb05 (analyst-baby 2026-05-31T08:04Z, Unicode escape pass) per git log on data/wins.json. Drift sourced from monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-31T01:14Z (max_drift=30.98% on tldr_verdict_length, bucket=tldr_only). OBE-skipped: WIN-007 (c4 review 2026-05-31T05:14Z newer than audit) and WIN-058 (c6 review 2026-05-31T07:13Z newer than audit). obe_skipped=2.
our_argument_summary: Std Model Explains. The c6/c7 verdict synthesis stands: Terra-Nova et al. (2017) PEPI 266:39-53 provides the spatial mechanism for SAA bifurcation via reversed-flux patches at the CMB; westward drift ~0.792 deg/yr (50.6 - 30.8 = 19.8 deg / 25 yr) is core-flow advection; African-American decay asymmetry is LLSVP-modulated. Dome H(r) = 8537*exp(-r/8619) km is purely radial and cannot reproduce azimuthal asymmetry. The post-hoc test fires independently: INTERMAGNET / ESA Swarm L1B / IGRF-14 were publicly indexed at the time of the dome's monitor commit, and the monitor.py target is hardcoded on the same commit as the data-fetch line. Two of three c7 holes have been resolved by post-c7 patches (data/wins.json:WIN-004); the third was the most severe and remains structurally open across a cohort of 4 sibling WINs.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt:14-15 (PROS-001 'exponential separation curve' label without tau)
raw-text/monitor.py (no SAA inter-cell domain found in 20-domain live-fetch suite)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Re-verified in c8 clone: monitor.py 20-domain live-fetch suite covers SAA decay rate (nT/yr) at INTERMAGNET stations (feeds WIN-037/042 family), NOT WIN-004's inter-cell angular separation. The 50.57 deg target lives as a static architectural constant in 05-model.txt; no monitor.py code path compares it against a live observation. Unchanged from c7.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded display. The dome's WIN list cites 50.6 deg as 'confirmed' but no calculation pipeline exists to refute the value if observation diverged. The c8 patches did not introduce new code paths.
data_sources_used: no live API for inter-cell angular separation
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — the WIN does not include adaptive tolerance because the comparison is not actually executed in monitor.py.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: No fresh discrepancies. The c7 code_analysis_tags survive c8 reverification.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-verified in c8 clone at run start. raw-text/05-model.txt:43 'SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg' remains a static architectural constant. The 20-domain live-fetch suite tracks INTERMAGNET nT/yr decay (WIN-037/042), not inter-cell angular separation (WIN-004). The post-c7 commits (bf3d721, 819cb05) modified data/wins.json prose only — no monitor.py path introduced.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Terra-Nova et al. (2017) PEPI 266:39-53 (DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002, re-verified resolvable in c8) remains the load-bearing globe-side citation: derives SAA bifurcation from reversed-flux patches and core flow, predates dome's prediction commits. The dome relabels the same mechanism 'aetheric vortex decay' without altering the predicted value. Current detail_evidence (1385 chars) retains the c5/c6/c7 framing.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020), INTERMAGNET, ESA Swarm L1B, IGRF-14 all publicly indexed and predate the dome monitor commit. The dome author's own definition ('prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled') is inapplicable because monitor.py hardcodes the target on the same commit as the data-fetch line. The c8 tldr_verdict rewrite preserves the parenthetical pointer to Part 3.1b post-hoc test and the Timestamp Error tab in plain-text form (closing the c7 hole #1 rendering bug for WIN-004 specifically).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Re-verified: PROS-001 (raw-text/02-wins.txt:14-15) still publishes 'exponential separation curve' label without tau. WIN-004 (raw-text/02-wins.txt:62-63) still confirms without derivation. detail_verdict_text at line ~6 retains 'the exponential rate parameter tau is unpublished'. The c8 detail_extra integration (PRED-013 feasibility) adds a quantitative forecast comparison but does NOT introduce a derivation path from disc radius / H(r) / aether coupling kappa — it's an extrapolation of the observed rate, not a dome-derived prediction.

holes_found.description: c7 hole #1 (major: TLDR HTML markup renders as escaped text) was closed via option (a) — per-WIN content fix for WIN-004 only. The systemic rendering bug at build-scripts/generate-html.js:828 and :840 (escapeHtml() on tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict) is still live, and the cohort identified by c7 is NOT fully patched: WIN-052 still ships escaped HTML in BOTH tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict (10, A tags); WIN-053 still ships an escaped link in tldr_verdict; WIN-064 still ships escaped every in tldr_evidence; and a fourth WIN c7 did not catch — WIN-070 — ships escaped HTML in tldr_evidence (data/wins.json fields verified, docs/index.html confirms literal <...> visible in rendered output for all four). No open ISS exists tracking either the systemic generate-html.js sanitizer change or the WIN-052/053/064 cohort patch. Closed-issues search shows only ISS-2291 (c7 hole #2) and ISS-2256 (c7 hole #3) were tracked — the most severe c7 hole was the least formally tracked. Risk: the per-WIN-004 fix is a content-only workaround; the next edit that introduces HTML in any tldr field will silently regress.
holes_found.recommendation:
Decider should open a new tracked issue with two-path options. (a) Cohort patch: rewrite WIN-052/053/064/070 tldr fields to plain text (mirror the WIN-004 c8 pattern — replace markup with parenthetical text references). Estimated 100-300 char edit per WIN, low risk, but leaves the build pipeline still vulnerable. (b) Systemic fix: at build-scripts/generate-html.js:828 and :840 (the tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict insertion sites in formatWinDetail), replace escapeHtml() with a curated allow-list sanitizer (, , , , ,
permitted; everything else escaped). Add tests/run.sh assertion grepping docs/index.html for `<a ` inside `class="ks-tldr"` blocks as a regression guard. This curmudgeon (c8) recommends (b) for the architectural durability reasons c7 already laid out, with (a) acceptable as an interim if (b) is deferred. Severity major because (i) c7's most-severe hole has not had a tracked closure, and (ii) at least 4 WINs (5 fields) ship visibly broken markup to readers right now, undermining the review's surface quality.

holes_found.description: Post-c7 rewrite of detail_evidence (commit 819cb05 or earlier) introduced awkward phrasing: 'is consistent with the drift of the South Atlantic anomaly westward regime modeled by core-flow MHD but is not a specific quantitative result of this citation.' The construction 'drift of the South Atlantic anomaly westward regime' is non-standard; readers parse 'drift of the SAA' then bump on 'westward regime' as a free-floating noun phrase. c7 hole #2's recommended softening was 'is consistent with the westward-drift advection regime modeled by core-flow MHD' — cleaner. The current text resolves ISS-2291 (the unsourced 'broader MHD models' phrasing is gone) but does so by introducing a stylistic clunker rather than adopting the c5/c7-recommended phrasing.
holes_found.recommendation: One-token reordering: replace 'is consistent with the drift of the South Atlantic anomaly westward regime modeled by core-flow MHD' with 'is consistent with the westward-drift advection regime modeled by core-flow MHD' — the c5 recommendation that ISS-2291 nominally was opened to apply. Single-sentence patch, zero downstream risk. Severity minor — the meaning is intact; this is a hygiene-grade polish, not an argument-weakening defect.

stronger_arguments.description: If the systemic sanitizer (Hole 1 recommendation b) lands, the WIN-004 tldr_verdict can be UPGRADED from parenthetical plain-text 'see Part 3.1b post-hoc test in the Self-Contradictions tab and the Timestamp Error tab' back to two functional cross-reference links. Both targets (Part 3.1b post-hoc test, the Timestamp Error tab) are the review's strongest methodological surfaces. Driving readers from a high-traffic TLDR into either of those tabs is a leverage multiplier — the systemic fix is the unblocker for restoring that leverage.
sources: c7 review WIN-004.c7.json hole #1 recommendation option (b)
current rendered docs/index.html for WIN-004 (TLDR is plain text)

kernel_of_truth.description: The SAA inter-cell angular separation IS a real measured quantity (raw-text/05-model.txt:43, 50.57 deg in 2025), and the dome model is genuinely committing to a forecast trajectory in PRED-013 — the new detail_extra integration (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_extra, post-c7 patch) acknowledges this on our side. The dome is not making up the observation; it is correctly citing a measurable angular separation between two real flux-patch centroids that the geomagnetic community tracks.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Acknowledging the observation is real does not validate dome cosmology. The PRED-013 feasibility check now in detail_extra (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_extra) makes this point quantitatively: linear extrapolation of the observed 0.792 deg/yr rate gives 74.4 deg by 2055, while PRED-013's lower bound (120 deg) requires 2.92x acceleration and the upper bound (180 deg) requires 5.45x — neither motivated by any dome-derived equation. The dome borrows the observation, labels the mechanism 'aetheric vortex decay,' and the LLSVP-modulated African-American asymmetry remains unaccounted for in any radially symmetric aether model. The kernel-of-truth defense (the observation is real) is identical for the standard model (which has a derived mechanism) and the dome (which does not) — so it provides zero discriminating support.

deeper_analysis: The pattern across c5 → c6 → c7 → c8 on WIN-004 illustrates a recurring decider behavior the operator should be aware of: when a curmudgeon review identifies a MAJOR architectural issue paired with several MINOR per-WIN issues, the decider's churn pipeline tends to close the minor items (which patch cleanly with a single sed/JSON edit) and defer the major architectural fix (which requires generate-html.js refactor + test plumbing). c7's hole #1 (major) explicitly offered two paths; the path the decider implicitly took was a third unstated option — patch WIN-004 only — which closes the symptom for one WIN while leaving the cohort and the architectural fragility intact. This c8 surfaces the cohort gap explicitly and recommends the systemic fix because no open ISS currently anchors the work. On the substantive science: WIN-004 remains in the same epistemic posture — Std Model Explains, supported by Terra-Nova et al. (2017) for spatial mechanism, by IGRF/CHAOS-7 for the quantitative kinematics, and now by the integrated PRED-013 feasibility math for the prospective-test interpretation. The 'is consistent with the drift of the SAA westward regime' phrasing in current detail_evidence resolves the c5/c6/c7 carry-over (ISS-2291) by removing the original problem phrase, though it would read more cleanly with the c5-recommended phrasing.

Finlay et al. (2020) Earth Planets Space — DOI 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 resolves correctly (used in WIN-005 sibling)
INTERMAGNET / ESA Swarm L1B / IGRF-14 — public datasets, no DOI needed

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender accepting the review's own methodological principles could argue: 'Your post-hoc test fails on its own internal-consistency requirement. You demand that for a prediction to count, the data must be 'not plausibly known to the author at write-time' — but Terra-Nova et al. (2017) is also publicly indexed and was known to the author when the review's standard-model claim was made. By the same standard you apply to the dome, your own MHD-mechanism argument is post-hoc. You are saying: when the dome cites a public dataset to confirm a mechanism it claims, that's post-hoc; when the review cites a public paper to refute a mechanism the dome claims, that's evidence. The asymmetric standard is the move your own Part 3.1b warns against.' The strongest version of this defense is rhetorically uncomfortable because it weaponizes our own framework against us.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The asymmetry is real and we address it implicitly but not explicitly. Standard-model claims do not need to be timestamped because they are not novelty claims — they are background mechanism citations. The dome is claiming a NOVEL prediction; the burden of pre-registration falls on the novelty claim, not the established mechanism. detail_verdict_text could add one sentence to make this asymmetry explicit: 'The post-hoc test applies to novelty claims (the dome's purported mechanism). Standard-model background citations are not held to the same temporal-priority bar because they are not asserting novel predictive content.' Not blocking — the defense survives at 2 (a fair-minded defender would land it but a sharp reader would see through it), so this is a polish opportunity not a hole. Lowered from c7's 3 because c7's defense was about defending the rendering-bug-fix and that scope is now closed; the remaining defense is the weaker asymmetric-burden argument.

related_wins: WIN-005 (African-American SAA decay asymmetry, detail_group WIN-004/005)
WIN-006 (North Pole drift 1600-1990, same MHD-core / post-hoc / hardcoded pattern)
cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-004/005/006 are internally consistent — same Terra-Nova et al. (2017) cite for the spatial mechanism, same dome-lacks-fluid-core argument, same INTERMAGNET / IGRF / CHAOS-7 post-hoc framing. WIN-006 additionally flags the 0.0466 deg/yr rate-reproduction failure and WIN-005 carries the factor-of-two decay differential — both reinforce WIN-004's case. No cross-WIN contradictions detected. ORTHOGONAL OBSERVATION: the TLDR-escape cohort (WIN-052/053/064/070) is not a WIN-004 cross-WIN consistency concern in the analytical sense, but it is a content-quality concern flagged in this review's holes_found[0].

claims_checked: 50.6 - 30.8 = 19.8 deg over 25 yr → 0.792 deg/yr westward bifurcation rate
Linear 2055 extrapolation: 50.6 + 30 * 0.792 = 74.36 deg (matches '74.4 deg' in detail_extra)
PRED-013 lower bound 120 deg: (120 - 50.6) / (30 * 0.792) = 69.4 / 23.76 = 2.921x (matches '2.92x' in detail_extra)
PRED-013 upper bound 180 deg: (180 - 50.6) / (30 * 0.792) = 129.4 / 23.76 = 5.446x (matches '5.45x' in detail_extra)
quantitative_verification.computations: All four PRED-013 feasibility numbers re-derived in c8: 50.6 - 30.8 = 19.8; 19.8 / 25 = 0.792; 50.6 + 30 * 0.792 = 74.36; (120-50.6)/(30*0.792) = 2.921; (180-50.6)/(30*0.792) = 5.446. Rounding to 1-2 decimal places gives exactly the values printed in data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_extra. The integration faithfully encodes the c4/c5/c6/c7 review-record math.
